violations.claim: Help text: "--since <ref>   Git ref to diff against (for derive)"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 557
; Help text: "--since <ref>   Git ref to diff against (for derive)"
; The "(for derive)" annotation implies only derive reads this flag.
; But args are forwarded to each command handler as-is; no command
; rejects flags it doesn't recognize. runInit/runAnalyze/etc. silently
; ignore --since because they never call getFlag(args, "--since").
(declare-const command_is_derive Int)     ; 1 = invoked command is "derive"
(declare-const since_in_args Int)         ; 1 = --since flag present in args
(declare-const since_consumed Int)        ; 1 = getFlag(args,"--since") called
(declare-const error_emitted Int)         ; 1 = command rejects unknown flag
; Code transition: runAnalyze never calls getFlag(args, "--since")
(assert (= command_is_derive 0))
(assert (= since_in_args 1))
(assert (=> (= command_is_derive 0) (= since_consumed 0)))
; Code transition: no command dispatches produce an "unknown flag" error
(assert (= error_emitted 0))
; Violation: user passes --since to a non-derive command; flag is silently lost
(assert (= since_in_args 1))
(assert (= since_consumed 0))
(assert (= error_emitted 0))
(check-sat)
